action.reasoning: Observation: tuning ensemble weights around the rank-label regression setup produced a new best hidden-test IC of 0.009361, with validation preferring a pure lgb+ridge blend and zero CatBoost weight. Analysis: the gain is small but real, and the validation-optimal weights suggest CatBoost may be adding noise under this target. A high-probability next refinement is to retrain the same rank-label pipeline with slightly stronger linear emphasis and more regularized/shallow trees, then retune weights on validation. This stays close to the only idea that has transferred to hidden test. Decision: patch training to support a rankreg_tuned mode with more conservative tree params and finer weight search, train a new checkpoint, test predict.py locally, and submit eval.
action.command: python train.py --checkpoint_dir checkpoints/rankreg_cs_tuned_v2 --label_mode rank --task regression --num_boost_round 600 --tune_weights --variant rankreg_tuned && mkdir -p tmp_eval && cp tmp_eval/eval_config.json tmp_eval/eval_config.json.bak 2>/dev/null || true && printf '{"test_period":["2021-01-01","2021-12-31"],"universe":"csi300","feature_set":"Alpha158"}' > tmp_eval/eval_config.json && python predict.py --data_path tmp_eval --checkpoint checkpoints/rankreg_cs_tuned_v2 --output tmp_eval/out10.json
